title: Cu-pa

The '''cu-pa''' was a [[Limb/Legends|bipedal]], [[Therapod|theropod-like]] [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[Reptomammal/Legends|reptomammal]] native to the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|worlds]] of [[Nam Chorios/Legends|Nam Chorios]] and [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]]. They were distant cousins of the [[Tauntaun/Legends|tauntaun]], another species of [[Diet/Legends|omnivorous]] reptomammals indigenous to the [[Water/Legends|icy]] planet of [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]]. However, unlike the tauntaun, the cu-pa had a more brightly [[Color/Legends|colored]] hide, and were not nearly as intelligent. However, they were very passive, and could be trained by [[Beastmaster/Legends|beast masters]] to act as mounts. The cu-pa was mostly comparable in size to the tauntaun, but a [[Subspecies/Legends|subspecies]] known as the wasteland cu-pa could grow to much larger sizes. [[Bioengineering/Legends|Bioengineers]] were also known to use the [[DNA/Legends|DNA]] of the cu-pa to incubate [[Mutant|mutated]] variants known as [[co-henpa]].

==Biology and appearance==
The [[Species/Legends|species]] of [[Limb/Legends|bipedal]], [[Therapod|theropod-like]] [[Reptomammal/Legends|reptomammals]] named cu-pa<ref name="AED" /> were native to the [[Outer Rim Territories/Legends|Outer Rim]] [[Planet/Legends|worlds]] of [[Nam Chorios/Legends|Nam Chorios]]<ref name="TCSWE" /> and [[Tatooine/Legends|Tatooine]].<ref name="RoD" /> The [[vertebrate]] species had brightly [[Color/Legends|colored]] bristly red hides in the wild, but when domesticated, their hides could come in a variety of different hues, including shades of yellow, green and blue.<ref name="AED" /> The cu-pa were distant cousins of the [[Tauntaun/Legends|tauntaun]],<ref name="TCSWE" /> another species of [[Diet/Legends|omnivorous]] reptomammals indigenous to the [[Water/Legends|icy]] planet of [[Hoth/Legends|Hoth]].<ref name="GG3">''[[Galaxy Guide 3: The Empire Strikes Back]]''</ref> However, the cu-pa was not nearly intelligent as their tauntaun counterparts.<ref name="PoT">''[[Planet of Twilight]]''</ref> Regardless, the cu-pa's passivity allowed for [[Beastmaster/Legends|beast masters]] to train cu-pa's as domesticated mounts.<ref name="RoD" /><ref name="AED" /> [[Subspecies/Legends|Subspecies]] of the cu-pa included the considerably larger [[wasteland cu-pa]], also native to Tatooine.<ref name="AED" />

Cu-pa meat was comestible. The cookbook ''[[Essential Cooking Skills]]'' detailed how to best cook that meat.<ref name="SWG:ECS">{{SWGcite|exp=AED|type=item|ESSENTIAL COOKING SKILLS}}</ref>

==History==
During the [[Time/Legends|time]] following the [[Battle of Yavin/Legends|Battle of Yavin]] in [[0 BBY/Legends|0 BBY]], miniature replicas of the cu-pa were available as house decorations. [[Bioengineering/Legends|Bioengineers]] were also successful in using the [[DNA/Legends|DNA]] of the cu-pa to incubate [[Mutant|mutated]] variants known as [[co-henpa]]. These mutated co-henpa exhibited a more scrawny, [[Reptile/Legends|reptilian-like]] appearance.<ref name="AED" /> In [[1 ABY/Legends|1 ABY]], a [[Gender/Legends|female]] [[Twi'lek/Legends|Twi'lek]] performer rode a pink and blue furred cu-pa during the grand opening of the [[Aerie Casino]] on [[Naboo/Legends|Naboo]]. The Human [[Xenobiologist/Legends|xenobiologist]] [[Dusque Mistflier]] looked on in fascination as the pale-blueskinned Twi'lek tightly wrapped her [[Lekku/Legends|lekku]] around the cu-pa's neck. Mistflier wondered if this allowed the performer to direct the animal's movement. Reserving her conclusions for a later date, the xenobiologist recorded her observations until she had more information.<ref name="RoD" />

==Behind the scenes==
The [[W:c:swg:Cu-pa|cu-pa]] first appeared in [[Barbara Hambly]]'s ''[[Planet of Twilight]]'', released in [[May]] of [[1997]], and later as a [[W:c:swg:Creatures & enemies|creature]] in the [[2003]] [[Star Wars video games|video game]] [[Star Wars Galaxies|''Star Wars Galaxies: An Empire Divided'']], a [[Roleplaying game|massively multiplayer online role-playing game]] developed by [[Sony]] Online Entertainment and published by [[LucasArts]], prior to its closure on [[December 15]], [[2011]].<ref name="AED" /><ref name="Shutdown">{{SonyCite|url=playerinfo|text=IMPORTANT INFORMATION ABOUT ''STAR WARS'' GALAXIES™}}</ref>

In addition to the wasteland cu-pa, there also existed other named types of cu-pa in [[W:c:swg:Star Wars Galaxies: An Empire Divided|''Galaxies'']], including the red cu-pa and wonderous cu-pa. However, both of these types of cu-pa were virtually identical in size and appearance to a typical cu-pa, so it is not clear if they constituted a distinct type of subspecies, or if they were merely descriptive names for different types of the same creature.<ref name="AED" /> While the cu-pa was first shown on Nam Chorios in both ''Planet of Twilight'' and ''[[The Essential Guide to Planets and Moons]]'',<ref name="PoT" /><ref name="PaM">''[[The Essential Guide to Planets and Moons]]''</ref> and is stated to be native to Nam Chorios in ''[[The Complete Star Wars Encyclopedia]]'',<ref name="TCSWE" /> ''[[Star Wars Galaxies: The Ruins of Dantooine]]'' states that the cu-pa was native to the [[W:c:swg:Planet|planet]] [[W:c:swg:Tatooine|Tatooine]].<ref name="RoD" />
